required_mitigations: Validate output file path to prevent directory traversal
Restrict output directory to safe location
Sanitize file path to remove '..' sequences
Use absolute path resolution and boundary checking
contract.notes: User-controlled file path used directly in file open operation without validation, allowing arbitrary file write access

required_mitigations: Validate jsonl_file path to prevent path traversal
Implement JSON schema validation for parsed data
Set limits on JSON parsing depth and object size
Sanitize parsed JSON data before database operations
Restrict file access permissions and validate file source
contract.notes: External JSON file content is deserialized without validation and passed to database operations. Malicious JSON could cause DoS or data corruption.

required_mitigations: Validate git_url_or_path format against URL/path regex
Use shell=False in subprocess.run (already done)
Sanitize special characters that could break argument parsing
Use a proper git library instead of subprocess
Implement allowlist of permitted git hosts/protocols
contract.notes: Command injection occurs in fallback path when git_utils import fails. Attacker can inject commands via malicious git URL.

required_mitigations: Validate jsonl_file path to prevent path traversal
Implement JSON schema validation for parsed data
Set limits on JSON parsing depth and object size
Sanitize parsed JSON data before database operations
Restrict file access permissions and validate file source
contract.notes: External JSON file content is deserialized without validation and passed to database operations. Malicious JSON could cause DoS or data corruption.

required_mitigations: URL encode the user parameter before interpolation
Validate AZURE_DEVOPS_USER contains only alphanumeric characters and allowed symbols
Use urllib.parse.quote() to properly escape special characters in URLs
Sanitize input to prevent characters that could break URL format (@, :, /, etc.)
contract.notes: Unvalidated username from environment variable is directly interpolated into git remote URL, potentially allowing URL injection or command manipulation

entrypoint: main.py::main(repo_path: str = ".", semgrep_config: str = None, base_ref: str = "origin/main", head_ref: str = "HEAD")::25

required_mitigations: Sanitize repo_path to remove path traversal sequences (../, \..\)
Validate repo_path is within expected directory boundaries
Resolve canonical path and verify it stays within allowed base directory
Use Path.resolve() and check resulting path is within safe bounds
contract.notes: Direct use of user input to construct Path object allows directory traversal attacks

required_mitigations: Use strncpy with proper size limit
Check input string length before copying
Use safer string functions like strlcpy or snprintf
contract.notes: Classic stack buffer overflow - strcpy copies unbounded input to fixed 16-byte buffer
